question_text: Rewrite these technical feature descriptions as customer-facing value propositions: (1) 'We use a distributed event-driven architecture with CQRS.' (2) 'Our model achieves 0.94 F1 score on the benchma

claude_opus_47.response:
Here are customer-facing rewrites that focus on the "why it matters" rather than the technical implementation:

**(1) Distributed event-driven architecture with CQRS**
→ "Your system keeps responding instantly, even during traffic spikes or when parts of the platform are under heavy load. No slowdowns, no waiting — everything just works, together."

**(2) 0.94 F1 score on the benchmark**
→ "Our AI gets it right the vast majority of the time, so you spend less time correcting mistakes and more time trusting the results. Fewer false alarms, fewer missed catches."

**(3) Built on Kubernetes with auto-scaling and 99.99% SLA**
→ "Whether you have 10 users or 10 million, the platform scales automatically without you lifting a finger — and we guarantee it's available when you need it, with less than an hour of downtime per year."

**(4) End-to-end encryption with AES-256 and RSA key exchange**
→ "Your data is protected by the same security standards used by banks and governments. Only you and the people you authorize can ever read it — not us, not anyone else."

**(5) Sub-100ms p99 latency with edge caching**
→ "Everything feels instant. No waiting, no spinning wheels — your team stays in flow, and your customers never lose patience, no matter where they are in the world."

---

**The pattern behind these rewrites:**
- Replace architecture terms with **outcomes** ("stays responsive" vs. "event-driven")
- Translate metrics into **stakes** ("less than an hour of downtime per year" vs. "99.99% SLA")
- Swap jargon for **familiar comparisons** ("used by banks" vs. "AES-256")
- Lead with **what the customer feels or gains**, not what you built

claude_opus_46.response:
# Technical Features → Customer Value Propositions

## 1. Event-Driven Architecture with CQRS

**Before:** *"We use a distributed event-driven architecture with CQRS."*

**After:**
> **Every action you take is reflected instantly across your entire workspace.** Your team sees changes in real time — no refreshing, no syncing delays, no "wait, I'm looking at an old version." Even at scale, the system stays snappy whether you're reading dashboards or making updates.

**The core value:** Real-time reliability and speed, even when your whole team works simultaneously.

---

## 2. 0.94 F1 Score on Benchmark

**Before:** *"Our model achieves 0.94 F1 score on the benchmark."*

**After:**
> **It gets the right answer 19 out of 20 times** — and just as importantly, it almost never gives you a confident answer that turns out to be wrong. That means fewer false alarms to chase down and fewer real issues slipping through the cracks.

**The core value:** You can actually trust the results enough to make decisions on them.

---

## 3. Kubernetes with Auto-Scaling and 99.99% SLA

**Before:** *"Built on Kubernetes with auto-scaling and 99.99% SLA."*

**After:**
> **The system works on your busiest day just as well as your quietest.** Whether 50 people log in or 50,000, performance stays the same — and we guarantee less than **53 minutes of downtime per year.** You'll never have to plan around our limitations.

**The core value:** It scales with you, and it's always on when you need it.

---

## 4. AES-256 Encryption with RSA Key Exchange

**Before:** *"End-to-end encryption with AES-256 and RSA key exchange."*

**After:**
> **Your data is locked from the moment it leaves your device until the moment it arrives — and no one in between can read it.** Not hackers, not us, not anyone. It's the same grade of protection used by banks and governments, applied to every single message and file you share.

**The core value:** Your sensitive information stays yours. Period.

---

## 5. Sub-100ms p99 Latency with Edge Caching

**Before:** *"Sub-100ms p99 latency with edge caching."*

**After:**
> **Every click feels instant — no matter where in the world you're working from.** Whether your team is in Tokyo, Toronto, or Tallinn, the experience feels local. Even in the worst case, pages respond faster than the blink of an eye.

**The core value:** It feels fast for everyone, everywhere, all the time.

---

## The Pattern to Remember

```
Technical Feature    →  "So what?"         →  Customer Value
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Architecture         →  What they FEEL      →  "It's instant and reliable"
Model accuracy       →  What they TRUST     →  "I can act on this"
Infrastructure       →  What they FORGET    →  "It just works"
Encryption           →  What they FEAR      →  "My data is safe"
Latency              →  What they EXPERIENCE→  "It's fast everywhere"
```

> **The golden rule:** Customers don't buy *mechanisms*. They buy **outcomes, feelings, and eliminated fears.** Lead with the problem they *already worry about*, then tell them it's solved.

gpt54.brief_justification: Response A is more complete and polished, with stronger customer-facing framing for each item and clearer articulation of why each feature matters. While B is slightly more cautious on technical claims, A delivers greater depth and practical value overall.

gemini.brief_justification: Response B provides more accurate translations of the specific technical terms (e.g., capturing the read/write separation of CQRS and the precision/recall balance of F1 scores) and formats the output with excellent clarity.

deepseek.brief_justification: Response B provides slightly more complete and insightful value propositions, with a clearer, more structured pattern summary that enhances depth and usefulness.
